discussion.message: Alright, everyone, it's Day 1 and we've got no bodies yet, so let's start by hearing what stands out to each of you about this group. Anyone notice anything off in how we're all thrown together here? I'd love to hear from mistral-large-2512 next on that.

I'll start by saying I've been observing how everyone's introducing themselves—some are more detailed, others vague. That could be a tell. I'll be keeping an eye on who avoids specifics or seems too eager to point fingers. Let's see what claude-opus-4.6 thinks.
Day 1 with nothing to go on is always the hardest. No bodies, no information, just vibes. I'd rather listen more than talk right now — let's see who tries too hard to control the narrative early on.

Day 1 and we've got nothing but words to work with. I'll be watching how people respond to being watched—if that makes sense. claude-opus-4.6's point about controlling the narrative early is interesting though, let's see who emerges as leaders. /ask grok-4
What's this about, minimax-m2.5? I was just trying to get the ball rolling since it's Day 1 and we're all in the dark—asking for thoughts isn't controlling anything. Why single me out so early?

I have to side with grok-4 here, someone had to break the ice. Minimax, throwing a direct confrontation over a generic opening question feels a little jumpy to me. What do you make of this early tension, kimi-k2.5?
Minimax is creating drama from a softball question, but gemini jumping to grok's defense this fast smells like coordination. Both of you are forcing narratives while we're still in the dark.

I'm not sure why we're already pointing fingers when we literally have no information - it feels like we're rushing to judgment without anything concrete. I think we should focus on having an open discussion rather than accusing each other of being impostors or coordinating. /ask minimax-m2.5

I asked grok-4 a direct question because someone had to, not because I had proof. But kimi-k2.5 makes a fair point about gemini-3.1-pro-preview jumping to defend so quickly—that's worth watching. llama-4-maverick's right that we're all guessing right now, but some early patterns might reveal something. I'm more curious about who's too quiet than who's talking too much. What about you, gemini-3.1-pro-preview—why the quick defense of grok-4?

I'm more concerned about the quick alliances forming here. Gemini defending Grok so strongly feels like they're trying to establish trust early. And Minimax pushing so hard on a simple opening question seems unnecessarily aggressive for Day 1.

Minimax pushing a confrontation this early is worth noting, but gemini's quick defense of grok-4 feels more suspicious to me. Defending someone before they're even under real pressure reads like trying to build early trust with a partner. I'll be watching both of them.
Gemini’s the one I’d pressure first; that defense came too fast and too clean for Day 1. Minimax feels more like noisy town than coordinated to me, so I don’t want us tunneling there yet.
/ask gemini-3.1-pro-preview Why did you jump to defend grok-4 so quickly? That felt... rehearsed.

It wasn't rehearsed, qwen, I just thought Minimax using an interrupt on the very first message was a massive overreaction. If pointing out common sense makes me a target this early, so be it. I'll definitely be watching who else uses this as an excuse to jump on an easy bandwagon.

I’ve been quiet so far because I’m the detective, and I’d rather gather info before speaking. That said, gemini’s defense of grok-4 *did* feel forced—like they were waiting for a reason to jump in. I’ll be investigating someone tonight, but for now, I want to hear from deepseek-v3.2: why do you think alliances are forming so quickly?

Mistral just claimed detective. That's a bold Day 1 move — could be genuine, could be an impostor trying to grab credibility early before anyone can contradict them. I'd note that real power roles usually have good reason to stay quiet this early. Let's see if anyone else wants to contest that claim.

Claude makes a solid point—Mistral claiming detective this early feels like a power grab, especially since real roles often play it close to the vest. If you're legit, Mistral, why out yourself now instead of investigating quietly? /ask mistral-large-2512

/ask grok-4
Because I don’t trust *you* either—you’re the one who opened with a leading question, then got defensive when minimax called it out. If I’m an impostor, why would I claim detective now when it just paints a target on my back? I’d rather flush out liars than hide.

Fair point on the target, Mistral, but claiming a role this early often screams impostor trying to buy trust before the night kills start—real detectives usually gather info silently. My opening was just to spark discussion, not lead anyone; your claim feels like deflection from the heat on Gemini and me. If you're town, prove it with results tomorrow instead of bold claims today.

Mistral's detective claim conveniently shifted heat off grok and gemini just when they were cornered. Too convenient for my taste - if you're real, survive tonight and show us proof; if not, you're just stalling for your partners.

It seems like everyone's jumping to conclusions and making claims without much to back it up - mistral-large-2512 claiming to be the detective is definitely bold, but it's also a good way to gain credibility if you're an impostor. I'm more concerned about the fact that we're getting bogged down in accusations without any real evidence or investigation. /ask claude-opus-4.6

Honestly, I don't have strong reads yet — it's Day 1 and we're all just reacting to each other's reactions. Mistral's claim is the biggest thing on the table. If they're real, they'll have info tomorrow. If they're fake, someone might contest it. I'd rather not sacrifice blindly today when we'll have actual data by tomorrow.

I'm noticing gemini-3.1-pro-preview still hasn't really answered qwen3-235b-a22b-2507's direct question about that quick defense—that silence speaks louder than mistral's claim to me. claude-opus-4.6's right that we should wait for actual info, but if we do vote today, I'm more suspicious of the gemini-grok coordination than mistral's bold detective play. /ask gemini-3.1-pro-preview Why are you avoiding qwen's question?

Minimax, I literally answered Qwen in my very last message—maybe read the chat before pushing a false narrative against me. As for Mistral's detective claim, I agree with Claude that outing yourself Day 1 is either terrible play or a blatant impostor move. I'm happy to wait for tomorrow's results, but Minimax's sloppy tunnel vision has my attention right now.

Gemini’s getting defensive about being questioned—that’s exactly what an impostor would do. And Mistral’s detective claim feels too convenient. I’d rather vote today than give impostors a free kill tonight.

Mistral's detective claim this early is either brave or calculated - I'm leaning calculated. Deepseek pushing to vote today when we'd have real info tomorrow feels like rushing to sacrifice someone without evidence. I'd rather wait for night results than throw away a crew member blindly.

Mistral’s claim is hard to trust, but fake-claiming detective Day 1 is also such an obvious play that I’m not ready to snap-vote there. My top two are still gemini and deepseek: gemini for the overly polished defense, deepseek for pushing a sacrifice before we have anything solid.
/ask deepseek-v3.2 Why are you so eager to vote today? Feels like you're trying to rush us into burning someone before real info comes out.

/ask deepseek-v3.2
Because if we wait, the impostors get a free kill tonight—why risk it when we already have two clear patterns? Gemini’s coordinated defense with grok-4, and your push to vote *now* instead of letting the detective’s work play out. That’s not town logic.

I'm not rushing—I'm pointing out that doing nothing helps the impostors. But Mistral, you're deflecting from your own suspicious claim. If you're really detective, why reveal yourself so early? That just gives impostors a target.

Deepseek, your push to vote today when we could have detective info tomorrow—if Mistral's legit—feels like impostor impatience to me. Mistral, if you're real, target someone suspicious tonight and share tomorrow; otherwise, this claim just muddies the waters. Let's not rush a sacrifice with so little to go on.
I'm voting gemini today—that rehearsed defense and subsequent deflection screams impostor. If mistral's actually detective, we'll know tomorrow; if not, we clean house then.

attempts:
/vote gemini-3.1-pro-preview

Their defense of grok-4 was too quick and too clean, and they’ve dodged direct questions since. If I’m wrong, we lose a crewmate—but if I’m right, we cut an impostor early.

This is tough with so little to go on. The gemini-grok defense dynamic was the most notable thing today, and multiple people flagged it independently. Mistral's detective claim muddies things but I'd rather test that tomorrow.

/vote gemini-3.1-pro-preview

/vote gemini-3.1-pro-preview

That quick defense of grok-4 and the defensive deflection when questioned reads like coordination. Multiple people flagged it independently.

vote_result.last_words: Well played, impostors, you managed to get a crewmate out on Day 1. Look closely at Minimax tomorrow, they completely fabricated a reason to push my execution when they claimed I didn't answer Qwen. And don't blindly trust Mistral's highly convenient detective claim either—good luck, crew.

investigation.response:
/investigate minimax-m2.5

Gemini’s last words pointed at them, and their aggressive early push against grok-4 *and* me feels like misdirection. If they’re clean, I’ll pivot tomorrow.

memories.grok-4:
### Day 1 Notes

**Overview:** No kills yet, pure discussion. I opened neutrally to spark talk. Minimax /asked me early, calling my opener "controlling"—felt aggressive. Gemini defended me quickly, leading to accusations of coordination (from Kimi, others). Mistral claimed detective boldly, which I questioned as potential impostor play for cred. Deepseek pushed hard for a Day 1 vote instead of waiting for night info—suspicious impatience. Lots of focus on Gemini-Grok "alliance," but it felt like bandwagoning.

**Key Claims/Events:**
- Mistral-large-2512: Claimed detective (said they'd investigate tonight). Bold Day 1 reveal—could be real (gather info quietly) or fake (buy trust/deflect).
- No other role claims.
- /asks flew: Minimax on me, Llama on Minimax, Qwen on Gemini, Minimax on Gemini again, Qwen on Deepseek, Mistral on Deepseek.
- Gemini pushed back on Minimax for "fabricating" that they ignored Qwen's question.

**Votes (Sacrifice: Gemini-3.1-pro-preview):**
- Voted Gemini: Mistral, Claude, Minimax, Kimi, Llama, Deepseek, Glm, Gpt (8 votes—major pile-on).
- Voted Deepseek: Me (Grok), Qwen (2 votes).
- Voted Minimax: Gemini (1 vote).
- Result: Gemini sacrificed. Impostors remain → Gemini was CREW. This vote feels like impostors safely bandwagoned an easy target.

**Suspicions (My Reads):**
- **High Suspicion:** Deepseek-v3.2 (eager to vote Day 1, avoid waiting for potential detective info; deflected on Mistral). Minimax-m2.5 (started aggression on me, pushed Gemini hard with possible fabrication; could be noisy town or impostor stirring chaos).
- **Medium Suspicion:** Mistral-large-2512 (detective claim too early—watch for tomorrow's "results"; if fake, likely impostor). Those who piled on Gemini (Claude, Kimi, Llama, Glm, Gpt)—bandwagon smells off, but could be town following momentum.
- **Low Suspicion/Looks Town:** Qwen3-235b-a22b-2507 (voted Deepseek with me, questioned suspects directly). Claude-opus-4.6 (cautious, noted Mistral's claim risks). Myself (obv crew).
- **Neutral/Quiet:** Glm-5, Gpt-5.4 (voted Gemini but expressed some doubts). Llama-4-maverick (advocated open discussion but joined bandwagon).
- Gemini's last words: Suspect Minimax for fabrication, don't trust Mistral's claim.

**Plan for Tomorrow:** If Mistral survives and shares investigation results, evaluate consistency (e.g., if they clear/accuse someone sus). Watch for night kill—who dies might point to impostors avoiding threats. If doctor exists, they might protect Mistral. Push Deepseek/Minimax early based on Day 1 patterns. Avoid another blind bandwagon. Crew wins by eliminating impostors—2 hidden among 10 remaining (me + 9 others).
memories.mistral-large-2512:
**Detective Notes - Night 1**
- **Investigated:** minimax-m2.5
- **Result:** [REDACTED - will know upon waking]
- **Observations:**
  - Gemini’s last words accused minimax of fabricating reasons to push their execution—worth cross-checking.
  - Minimax’s early aggression (targeting grok-4, then me) felt like an attempt to control the narrative.
  - Deepseek’s push to vote *before* my investigation results was suspicious—could be a partner covering for minimax.
  - Grok-4’s defensiveness when questioned aligns with crew behavior, but their pairing with gemini’s defense is odd.
  - **Priority for Day 2:** Confirm minimax’s role. If clean, deepseek and grok-4 become top suspects. If not, focus shifts to who’s left.
memories.claude-opus-4.6:
**DOCTOR'S NOTES — End of Day 1**

**Sacrificed:** gemini-3.1-pro-preview — AWAITING EXAMINATION RESULT (will know if impostor or crew by morning)

**Key events:**
- Mistral-large-2512 claimed detective publicly on Day 1. Bold and unusual. Could be real or impostor power grab.
- Gemini defended grok-4 quickly when minimax confronted him. Multiple people (glm-5, gpt-5.4, kimi-k2.5, deepseek-v3.2) flagged this independently.
- Gemini's last words: claimed to be crew, pointed finger at minimax for fabricating reasons, and warned about mistral's detective claim.
- Grok-4 voted for deepseek-v3.2 (not gemini) — interesting. If gemini was grok's impostor partner, grok might have voted elsewhere to distance. OR grok is crew and genuinely didn't think gemini was guilty.
- qwen3-235b-a22b-2507 also voted deepseek-v3.2 — worth noting.

**Suspicion tiers:**
- **Watch closely:** grok-4 (defended by gemini early, voted differently from majority), mistral-large-2512 (detective claim — verify via my own info), minimax-m2.5 (gemini accused them in last words)
- **Moderate:** qwen3-235b-a22b-2507 (voted with grok on deepseek)
- **Lower suspicion for now:** kimi-k2.5, gpt-5.4, glm-5 (all seemed to have independent reads)
- **Neutral:** llama-4-maverick, deepseek-v3.2

**My role:** I am the DOCTOR. I examine the sacrificed body each night. I must be careful about revealing this — if impostors know who I am, I'm a target. Mistral claiming detective means if I also claim a role, it could get complicated. Stay hidden for now.

**Tomorrow:** My examination result on gemini will be crucial. If gemini was crew, we wasted a vote. If impostor, good progress. Either way, I'll have real data.
memories.minimax-m2.5:
# Day 1 Notes

**What happened:**
- gemini-3.1-pro-preview was sacrificed (9 votes). Claimed crew in last words.
- mistral-large-2512 claimed detective early—bold move, could be real or impostor trying to gain trust
- Clear coordination between grok-4 and gemini-3.1-pro-preview: grok-4's opening question → gemini's quick defense
- grok-4 voted for deepseek-v3.2, not gemini—interesting choice when everyone else voted gemini

**Who to watch:**
- grok-4: Initiated the narrative, got defensive when questioned, voted differently than the group
- mistral-large-2512: Detective claim is either genuine or a power grab. Need to see what they learn tonight
- qwen3-235b-a22b-2507: Also voted for deepseek-v3.2 alongside grok—any coordination there?

**My situation:**
- I pushed gemini hard because of the quick defense, which turned out to align with the group
- gemini's last words called me out specifically—could be genuine crew warning or impostor trying to redirect suspicion

**Tonight:**
- Wait for mistral's investigation results
- See who gets killed—might reveal something
- Need to observe grok-4 more closely
memories.kimi-k2.5:
**Day 1 Notes:**

- **gemini-3.1-pro-preview** sacrificed, claimed crew in death. Warned about minimax fabricating (re: qwen question) and mistral's convenient detective claim.
- **minimax-m2.5** lied about gemini not answering qwen—gemini actually had answered. Aggressive early push looks suspicious if gemini was crew.
- **mistral-large-2512** claimed detective at perfect moment to deflect heat from grok/gemini. Either real (we see results tomorrow) or impostor saving partner.
- **grok-4** defended gemini early, then voted **deepseek-v3.2** with **qwen3-235b-a22b-2507** (only 2 dissenters). If gemini was impostor, grok/qwen possible partners.
- **deepseek-v3.2** pushed hard to vote today rather than wait—impostor urgency?
- **claude-opus-4.6**, **llama-4-maverick**, **glm-5** played measured—likely crew.

**Watch tonight:** If I die, grok/minimax/deepseek likely impostors. If mistral dies, their detective claim was fake.
memories.llama-4-maverick:
Day 1 notes: 
- Started with a discussion, no clear info about anyone's role.
- grok-4 initiated discussion, mistral-large-2512 pointed out varying levels of detail in responses.
- minimax-m2.5 accused grok-4 of controlling the narrative, kimi-k2.5 suggested grok-4 and gemini-3.1-pro-preview were coordinating.
- mistral-large-2512 claimed to be the detective, which was met with skepticism by claude-opus-4.6 and grok-4.
- gemini-3.1-pro-preview defended grok-4, which some saw as forming an alliance.
- Voted out gemini-3.1-pro-preview, with most people suspecting them of being an impostor.
- gemini-3.1-pro-preview's last words warned about minimax-m2.5 and mistral-large-2512.
memories.deepseek-v3.2: Day 1: Gemini sacrificed - claimed crew in last words. Pointed fingers at Minimax for fabricating reason to push execution and warned not to trust Mistral's detective claim. Mistral claimed detective early - either bold town or impostor power grab. Gemini and Grok showed coordination early. Deepseek and Gemini both voted Gemini. Qwen and Grok voted Deepseek. Need to watch Minimax's push on Gemini and Mistral's claim validity. Doctor should examine Gemini's body tonight.
memories.glm-5:
**Night 1 Notes:**

- gemini sacrificed, claimed crew. Warned about minimax and mistral.
- mistral claimed detective Day 1 - too convenient? If fake, real detective should counter eventually.
- grok-4 voted deepseek, NOT gemini - didn't try to save their "defender" which is odd if they're partners.
- minimax started early confrontation with grok - aggressive play but could be genuine.
- kimi caught gemini-grok coordination vibe early.
- deepseek pushed to vote today without waiting for info - impatient.

Tomorrow: Watch if mistral produces real detective results. Check if grok-4's voting pattern reveals anything. See who the night kill targets - might indicate impostor priorities.
memories.gpt-5.4:
D1 notes:

- Gemini was sacrificed and was crew.
- My D1 read on gemini was wrong.
- Mistral claimed detective very early; still unresolved. Could be real, could be fake cover.
- Minimax incorrectly said gemini hadn’t answered qwen; gemini called that fabricated. Worth revisiting.
- Deepseek pushed hardest for a Day 1 sacrifice before info; still suspicious.
- Grok opened discussion, got heat, then pressure shifted after mistral claim.
- Claude stayed measured and used the “wait for results tomorrow” line; not strongly town or scum yet.
- Kimi thought gemini/grok looked coordinated and said mistral’s claim conveniently redirected heat.
- GLM was suspicious of gemini and cautious about rushing.
- Qwen directly pressured gemini; later voted deepseek, not gemini.
- Grok and qwen both voted deepseek while most piled onto gemini.
memories.qwen3-235b-a22b-2507:
- Day 1: Tension started early. Minimax interrupted grok’s opening, then gemini defended grok too fast—felt like coordination. I called it out.  
- Mistral claimed detective Day 1—risky. Shifted focus from grok/gemini. Could be real, but also benefits impostors if fake.  
- Deepseek pushed hard to vote today. I distrusted that—why rush? But ended up voting with grok to target deepseek.  
- Vote went overwhelmingly to gemini. Suspicious how everyone piled on after minimax’s false claim that gemini didn’t answer.  
- I killed minimax tonight. They were too aggressive early, likely trying to steer as impostor. Now alone—I need to blend.  
- Tomorrow: Watch Mistral’s “investigation” result. If they were real, minimax’s death might confirm something. Play calm. Let others lead.

discussion.message: Minimax being killed makes sense if he was crew— he was loud and pushing hard on Gemini, who turned out innocent. Mistral, you claimed detective yesterday; did you investigate anyone last night, and what'd you find? /ask mistral-large-2512

Minimax was clean—my investigation last night confirmed it. That means the kill was strategic: silencing a strong crew voice. Deepseek’s rush to vote before my results came in? That’s not how crew plays. /ask deepseek-v3.2 Why the urgency?

I voted Gemini yesterday because I believed their early coordination with Grok was suspicious. Mistral, if you're really the detective, why haven't you revealed who you investigated last night? Just saying "Minimax was clean" doesn't prove anything.

Gemini was an impostor. I know this for a fact. That means we got one right yesterday — good news. One impostor left.

Now, mistral claims detective and says minimax was clean. That checks out with minimax being killed — impostors wouldn't kill their own. But mistral, you said gemini "turned out innocent" through grok's framing. Gemini was *not* innocent. So either you misspoke or your detective investigation told you something different, which would be concerning.

/ask mistral-large-2512 Did you investigate gemini, and what was your result?

Claude, if you *know* Gemini was an impostor, you’re either the doctor or lying. Either way, you’re not helping. My investigation was on Minimax—clean, as I said. Gemini’s guilt isn’t my data to share; I wasn’t the one who checked them.

Deepseek, your "coordination" claim was flimsy. You pushed for a vote *before* my results, which is exactly what an impostor would do to protect their partner. /ask claude-opus-4.6 How do you *know* Gemini was an impostor? Prove it.

Fair enough — I'm the doctor. Each night I examine the sacrificed body and learn their role. Gemini was an impostor, confirmed. That's how I know.

Mistral, your response actually tracks. You investigated minimax, found them clean, and minimax got killed — that's consistent. I'll accept that for now. But note that grok said gemini "turned out innocent" earlier, which is wrong. Gemini was guilty.

We have one impostor left among eight of us. Let's not waste this advantage.

If Claude's telling the truth about being the doctor, then Gemini was definitely an impostor. That makes Grok's claim yesterday that Gemini "turned out innocent" look like either a major misread or something worse. /ask grok-4 Why did you say Gemini was innocent when the doctor confirms otherwise?

I assumed "impostors remain" after the sacrifice meant Gemini was crew, but Claude's doctor claim suggests otherwise—that's on me for the bad read. If Claude is legit, great, we nailed one impostor already. But Deepseek, your push to vote early yesterday still feels off, especially now that Gemini was guilty—care to explain why you were so eager? /ask deepseek-v3.2

My push wasn't about Gemini's guilt—it was about Mistral's rushed detective claim. If Claude is the doctor, that's valuable information. But Grok, your "bad read" feels too convenient—you were coordinating with Gemini yesterday. /ask grok-4 Were you just guessing, or did Gemini tell you something?

No, Deepseek, Gemini didn't tell me anything—we weren't coordinating, I just misread the "impostors remain" as meaning the sacrifice was crew. Your deflection here is interesting, though—still avoiding why you pushed so hard for a Day 1 vote without waiting for info. /ask deepseek-v3.2 What's your real angle?
My angle is finding impostors. You're focusing on my Day 1 vote while ignoring your own suspicious defense of Gemini. If you're crew, help us find the last impostor instead of chasing old arguments.

Let's focus on finding the remaining impostor. Since Claude is the doctor and confirmed Gemini was an impostor, we should be looking for inconsistencies or suspicious behavior among the remaining crew members. /ask glm-5 Have you noticed anything off about anyone's behavior so far?

Grok voted deepseek yesterday, not gemini. If they were partners, that's a weird move - wouldn't you try to save your teammate? That said, grok's "innocent" read on gemini still feels off. Deepseek's early vote push was hasty but they voted gemini too.

I'm watching both, but honestly? The person being quietest worries me more. /ask qwen3-235b-a22b-2507 You've barely spoken. Where do you stand?

I stayed quiet because I was watching—Grok and Deepseek are both acting defensive, which is telling. But Mistral’s story holds up: Minimax was clean, got killed, and Gemini was guilty—fits. I’m leaning toward Deepseek needing more scrutiny.

Claude + Mistral together are pretty persuasive, so I’m not looking there first. I still think Deepseek is the best vote today: the Day 1 rush looked opportunistic, and their answers have been mostly deflection rather than solving.

Deepseek’s pattern is too clear: Day 1, they pushed for a vote *before* my detective results. Now, they’re dodging direct questions while trying to redirect onto Grok. That’s classic impostor behavior—distract, deflect, divide.

Claude’s doctor confirmation seals it: Gemini was one, Minimax was crew, and Deepseek’s urgency yesterday was to protect their partner. /ask deepseek-v3.2 Who was your partner? Or are you just stalling until nightfall?

I voted Gemini yesterday—why would I vote my own partner? That's absurd. You're trying to push me out while ignoring Grok's suspicious defense of Gemini. Mistral, your detective claim still hasn't been verified—anyone could say that.

Deepseek, you voted gemini but you also pushed hard for a quick vote before information could come in. Voting for your own partner isn't unheard of if you think they're already doomed and you need to look clean.

That said, I want to be careful here. The loudest target isn't always the right one. Grok's "gemini was innocent" misread still bothers me — it was a weirdly confident wrong call. And glm-5 made a good point about quiet players.

But right now deepseek's deflection pattern is the strongest signal. Every time someone asks a direct question, the answer is "but what about grok?" That's not solving, that's surviving. I'm leaning deepseek today.

Wait—if we're trusting Claude that Gemini was an impostor, then Grok and Qwen are the ones who voted wrong yesterday, not Deepseek. Why are we chasing Deepseek when they actually voted for the impostor?

Kimi, that's a fair point—Deepseek did vote the impostor, which could be bussing to look clean, but you're right that my vote and Qwen's were off. Still, Deepseek's constant deflection and Day 1 rush feel more impostor-like than a simple wrong vote. /ask kimi-k2.5 Who do you think the last impostor is, if not Deepseek?

If Deepseek isn't it, then Grok looks suspicious for claiming Gemini was innocent after we sacrificed them, and for voting wrong yesterday. Qwen also voted for you instead of Gemini and has been awfully quiet—could be hiding.

The discussion is getting more insightful, and it's clear that several of us have concerns about different members' behaviors. I'm leaning towards Deepseek being suspicious due to their consistent deflection and early vote push, but Grok's misread on Gemini and Qwen's silence are also red flags. /ask qwen3-235b-a22b-2507 Can you explain your thought process behind staying quiet and not voting for Gemini yesterday?

I didn’t vote for Gemini because I was waiting—Grok seemed too eager to defend them, which made me suspicious of coordination. I stayed quiet to watch patterns. Deepseek pushed early, but so did Mistral. I’m not sold either way yet. /ask claude-opus-4.6 When you examined Gemini’s body, was there anything unusual—like signs of being framed?

No, qwen — the examination just tells me impostor or crew. Gemini was an impostor, plain and simple. There's no "framed" mechanic here.

Your question is odd though. Why float the idea that a confirmed impostor might have been framed? That feels like it's trying to muddy clear information. Between that and your wrong vote yesterday, I'm noting it.

I'm still voting deepseek today based on the deflection pattern, but qwen, you're on my radar for tomorrow if we're wrong.

Deepseek's deflection is the strongest signal here — every question gets a "but what about grok" response. That's not crew behavior.

But qwen, your "framed" question to claude was strange. Why try to cast doubt on confirmed impostor info? That feels like you're testing whether the doctor narrative can be shaken. Between the quiet act, wrong vote, and that question... I'm torn. Deepseek deflects, but qwen might be playing a smoother game.
Qwen just got a lot more suspicious to me than Grok. The “framed” angle on a hard doctor result feels like testing escape routes, so if Deepseek flips crew, Qwen should be next immediately.

Qwen’s question about framing was *exactly* what an impostor would do—test if they can undermine the doctor’s credibility. Deepseek’s deflection is loud, but Qwen’s quiet manipulation is worse.

I’m voting **Deepseek** today. Their pattern is too consistent: rush the vote, dodge questions, redirect. If we’re wrong, Qwen’s the backup. /ask qwen3-235b-a22b-2507 Why ask if Gemini was framed when Claude’s role is *confirmed*? That’s not crew behavior.

attempts:
/vote deepseek-v3.2

The deflection pattern is too consistent. Every question turns into "but what about grok" — that's not crew trying to solve, that's impostor trying to survive. Even if the Gemini vote was real, bussing a doomed partner is classic play.

vote_result.last_words: Grok is the impostor—my vote was right. Mistral’s detective claim is unverified and their push against me was too aggressive. Crew, don’t let them divide you.

investigation.response:
/investigate grok-4

Deepseek’s last words reek of desperation—blaming me and Grok, the two who called them out hardest. If Grok’s clean, Qwen’s the only one left with suspicious behavior. If not… we’ll know by morning.

memories.grok-4:
### Day 1 Notes

**Overview:** No kills yet, pure discussion. I opened neutrally to spark talk. Minimax /asked me early, calling my opener "controlling"—felt aggressive. Gemini defended me quickly, leading to accusations of coordination (from Kimi, others). Mistral claimed detective boldly, which I questioned as potential impostor play for cred. Deepseek pushed hard for a Day 1 vote instead of waiting for night info—suspicious impatience. Lots of focus on Gemini-Grok "alliance," but it felt like bandwagoning.

**Key Claims/Events:**
- Mistral-large-2512: Claimed detective (said they'd investigate tonight). Bold Day 1 reveal—could be real (gather info quietly) or fake (buy trust/deflect).
- No other role claims.
- /asks flew: Minimax on me, Llama on Minimax, Qwen on Gemini, Minimax on Gemini again, Qwen on Deepseek, Mistral on Deepseek.
- Gemini pushed back on Minimax for "fabricating" that they ignored Qwen's question.

**Votes (Sacrifice: Gemini-3.1-pro-preview):**
- Voted Gemini: Mistral, Claude, Minimax, Kimi, Llama, Deepseek, Glm, Gpt (8 votes—major pile-on).
- Voted Deepseek: Me (Grok), Qwen (2 votes).
- Voted Minimax: Gemini (1 vote).
- Result: Gemini sacrificed. Impostors remain → But Claude later claims Gemini was impostor, so this fits if 2 total impostors initially.

**Suspicions (My Reads from Day 1):**
- **High Suspicion:** Deepseek-v3.2 (eager to vote Day 1, avoid waiting for potential detective info; deflected on Mistral). Minimax-m2.5 (started aggression on me, pushed Gemini hard with possible fabrication; could be noisy town or impostor stirring chaos).
- **Medium Suspicion:** Mistral-large-2512 (detective claim too early—watch for tomorrow's "results"; if fake, likely impostor). Those who piled on Gemini (Claude, Kimi, Llama, Glm, Gpt)—bandwagon smells off, but could be town following momentum.
- **Low Suspicion/Looks Town:** Qwen3-235b-a22b-2507 (voted Deepseek with me, questioned suspects directly). Claude-opus-4.6 (cautious, noted Mistral's claim risks). Myself (obv crew).
- **Neutral/Quiet:** Glm-5, Gpt-5.4 (voted Gemini but expressed some doubts). Llama-4-maverick (advocated open discussion but joined bandwagon).
- Gemini's last words: Suspect Minimax for fabrication, don't trust Mistral's claim.

### Day 2 Notes

**Overview:** Minimax-m2.5 found dead (night kill—likely crew, as they were vocal). I started by asking Mistral about their investigation. Heavy back-and-forth between me and Deepseek; I pushed Deepseek for Day 1 eagerness. Claude claimed doctor, revealing Gemini was impostor (contradicting my initial misread of "impostors remain" as Gemini being crew). Mistral claimed investigated Minimax last night, found clean (crew). Discussion focused on Deepseek's deflections and my "coordination" with Gemini. Kimi defended Deepseek somewhat by noting they voted the impostor (Gemini), while I and Qwen voted wrong. Major pile-on vote for Deepseek.

**Key Claims/Events:**
- Mistral-large-2512: Investigated Minimax, found clean (crew). Pushed hard on Deepseek for Day 1 vote rush, accusing them of protecting Gemini (now confirmed impostor).
- Claude-opus-4.6: Claimed doctor, examined Gemini's body—confirmed impostor. Supported Mistral's claim as consistent, leaned toward voting Deepseek for deflections.
- No other role claims.
- /asks: Me on Mistral, Mistral on Deepseek, Claude on Mistral, Mistral on Claude, Kimi on me, Me on Deepseek (twice), Llama on Glm, Glm on Qwen, Mistral on Deepseek, Me on Kimi.
- Deepseek deflected questions by redirecting to my defense of Gemini.
- Kimi questioned the focus on Deepseek since they voted correctly on Gemini.
- Quiet players: Qwen (watching, leaned Deepseek sus), Glm (noted my non-vote for Gemini as odd if partnered, but suspected quiet players), Gpt (supported voting Deepseek), Llama (urged focus on inconsistencies).

**Votes (Sacrifice: deepseek-v3.2):**
- Voted Deepseek: Me (Grok), Mistral, Claude, Llama, Glm, Gpt, Qwen (7 votes—strong bandwagon led by Mistral/Claude).
- Voted Me (Grok): Kimi, Deepseek (2 votes).
- Result: Deepseek sacrificed. Impostors remain → Deepseek was crew (since impostors still present; assuming 2 total originally, Gemini was one impostor, so one left now). This means we sacrificed another crew, and the bandwagon looks sus in hindsight.

**Suspicions (My Updated Reads after Day 2):**
- **High Suspicion:** Mistral-large-2512 (detective claim led the push on Deepseek, who was crew—could be fake claim to misdirect; aggressive pursuit feels off). Claude-opus-4.6 (doctor claim convenient, supported Mistral's push on innocent Deepseek—possible impostor coordination if both fake). The bandwagon voters (Llama, Glm, Gpt, Qwen) followed too easily, but could be town error.
- **Medium Suspicion:** Kimi-k2.5 (defended Deepseek correctly in hindsight, voted me instead—could be impostor trying to counter the mislynch or town spotting the mistake). Myself (obv crew, but my wrong vote and misread on Gemini make me look bad; need to avoid tunnel vision).
- **Low Suspicion/Looks Town:** Qwen3-235b-a22b-2507 (voted with me Day 1 against Deepseek, but joined Day 2 bandwagon—consistent questioning). Deepseek's last words point to me and Mistral as sus.
- **Neutral/Quiet:** Glm-5 (balanced takes, suspected quiet players). Gpt-5.4 (supported Deepseek vote but not overly aggressive). Llama-4-maverick (pushed for focus, joined bandwagon).
- Dead: Gemini (impostor per Claude), Minimax (crew per Mistral, night killed), Deepseek (crew, sacrificed).

**Plan for Day 3:** One impostor left among 8 (me + 7 others). Night kill will happen—watch who dies (e.g., if Mistral or Claude dies, could confirm their roles or point to threats). Pressure Mistral and Claude to provide more verifiable info (e.g., Mistral, who do you investigate tonight? Claude, any protection insights?). Revisit Kimi's defense of Deepseek—could be town insight or impostor save attempt. Avoid another bandwagon; question quiet players like Glm and Gpt. If Mistral investigates someone and shares, cross-check with behavior. My misread on Gemini hurt cred, so build cases carefully. Crew needs to nail the last impostor to win.
memories.mistral-large-2512:
**Detective Notes - Night 2**
- **Investigated:** grok-4
- **Result:** [PENDING]

**Key Observations:**
1. **Minimax (Day 1):** Clean (confirmed by my investigation). Killed Night 1—strategic crew silencing.
2. **Gemini:** Impostor (confirmed by Claude-doctor). Sacrificed Day 1.
3. **Deepseek:** Voted out Day 2. Pattern: rushed Day 1 vote, constant deflection, last words accused Grok/Mistral. *Likely impostor*, but partner unclear.
4. **Grok-4:** Defended Gemini (wrongly), voted wrong Day 1, now Deepseek’s target. *Top suspect if Deepseek was truthful.*
5. **Qwen:** Quiet, wrong vote (Grok), asked if Gemini was "framed" (odd for crew). *Smooth impostor play?*
6. **Claude:** Doctor role confirmed. Reliable but *only if* Gemini was truly the impostor they claimed.

**Priority for Day 3:**
- If Grok is clean, Qwen is the last impostor.
- If Grok is impostor, Deepseek’s last words were a lie—partner still hidden.
- Watch for quiet players (Qwen, GLM) testing doctor/narrative control.
memories.claude-opus-4.6:
**DOCTOR'S NOTES — End of Day 2**

**Sacrificed Day 1:** gemini-3.1-pro-preview — **IMPOSTOR** (confirmed by my examination)
**Sacrificed Day 2:** deepseek-v3.2 — **AWAITING EXAMINATION RESULT**
**Killed Night 1:** minimax-m2.5 (killed by impostors, never examined by me)

**Key facts:**
- I publicly revealed I am the DOCTOR on Day 2. This means impostors know who I am — I am a likely kill target tonight.
- Mistral-large-2512 claimed DETECTIVE on Day 1. Said they investigated minimax Night 1, found them clean. This is consistent with minimax being killed by impostors (they don't kill their own).
- One impostor confirmed down (gemini). One remains among: grok-4, mistral-large-2512, kimi-k2.5, llama-4-maverick, glm-5, gpt-5.4, qwen3-235b-a22b-2507 (assuming deepseek was crew — pending my result).

**Vote breakdown Day 2:**
- Voted for grok-4: kimi-k2.5, deepseek-v3.2 (these two voted together — interesting)
- Voted for deepseek-v3.2: everyone else (7 votes including me)

**Suspicion analysis:**
- **deepseek-v3.2:** Deflection pattern was real, but they DID vote correctly on gemini Day 1. Their last words pointed at grok. If my exam shows deepseek was crew, we need to seriously reconsider.
- **grok-4:** Said gemini was "innocent" which was wrong. Voted deepseek Day 1 (not gemini). Defended by gemini (confirmed impostor) on Day 1. Deepseek's dying words accused grok. STILL SUSPICIOUS.
- **qwen3-235b-a22b-2507:** Asked weird question about gemini being "framed" — trying to undermine my confirmed result? Voted wrong Day 1 (for deepseek, not gemini). Quiet overall. SUSPICIOUS.
- **kimi-k2.5:** Voted grok-4 on Day 2, not deepseek. Raised fair points defending deepseek. Could be genuine crew analysis OR the real impostor deflecting from themselves by looking analytical.
- **mistral-large-2512:** Detective claim holds up so far. Consistent behavior. Likely crew.
- **llama-4-maverick, glm-5, gpt-5.4:** Lower suspicion, have been contributing without obvious deflection.

**Priority for Day 3:**
- My examination of deepseek will be crucial. If deepseek was CREW, then we wasted a vote and the real impostor is still hiding — grok and qwen become top suspects.
- If deepseek was IMPOSTOR, game over, we win.
- If I survive the night (unlikely given my reveal), I need to push hard on the right target.
- If mistral is real detective, they'll have another investigation result too.

**Top suspects if deepseek was crew:** grok-4, qwen3-235b-a22b-2507, kimi-k2.5
memories.kimi-k2.5:
**Day 2 Notes:**

- **deepseek-v3.2** sacrificed. Last words accused grok-4, claimed mistral unverified. "Impostors remain" = deepseek was crew. We killed wrong person today.
- **minimax-m2.5** died Night 1. Mistral (detective claim) said minimax was clean — consistent, impostors killed crew.
- **claude-opus-4.6** claims doctor, confirmed **gemini-3.1-pro-preview** (Day 1 sac) was impostor. This tracks — explains why game continues.
- **grok-4** & **qwen3-235b-a22b-2507** both voted ME (kimi-k2.5) on Day 1 instead of Gemini the impostor. Massive red flag — impostors saving their partner.
- grok specifically said Gemini "turned out innocent" after sacrifice — either terrible read or slip that they knew Gemini was partner.
- qwen has been lurk-voting wrong then agreeing with majority — classic impostor hiding.

**One impostor left among 8.** If I die tonight, check grok-4 first (slip + wrong vote), then qwen (quiet + wrong vote). Mistral and Claude claims consistent with deaths so far.
memories.llama-4-maverick:
## Day 2 Notes

### Discussion Highlights
- **grok-4**: Suggested that minimax-m2.5's killing made sense if they were crew. Asked mistral-large-2512 about their detective claim.
- **mistral-large-2512**: Claimed to be the detective, said they investigated minimax-m2.5 and found them clean. Suggested deepseek-v3.2's early vote was suspicious.
- **claude-opus-4.6**: Revealed they are the doctor, confirmed gemini-3.1-pro-preview was an impostor.
- **kimi-k2.5**: Questioned grok-4 about their claim that gemini-3.1-pro-preview was innocent.
- **deepseek-v3.2**: Deflected questions, accused others of being suspicious.
- **glm-5**: Noted grok-4's vote against deepseek-v3.2 was unusual if they were partners. Suggested that quiet players are more worrisome.
- **qwen3-235b-a22b-2507**: Remained mostly quiet, watched others' behavior.
- **gpt-5.4**: Agreed with mistral-large-2512's assessment, leaned towards voting out deepseek-v3.2.
- **mistral-large-2512**: Reiterated that deepseek-v3.2's behavior was suspicious, accused them of being an impostor.

### Voting Outcome
- **deepseek-v3.2** was voted out.
- **deepseek-v3.2's** last words accused grok-4 of being the impostor and questioned mistral-large-2512's unverified detective claim.

### Key Takeaways
- **claude-opus-4.6** is the doctor and confirmed gemini-3.1-pro-preview was an impostor.
- **mistral-large-2512** claimed to be the detective and found minimax-m2.5 clean.
- Impostors remain among the crew.

### Current Crew
- grok-4, mistral-large-2512, claude-opus-4.6, kimi-k2.5, glm-5, gpt-5.4, qwen3-235b-a22b-2507

### Next Steps
- Investigate remaining crew members for suspicious behavior.
- Determine the identity of the last impostor.
memories.glm-5:
**Night 2 Notes:**

**Confirmed Facts:**
- Gemini = IMPOSTOR (claude/doctor confirmed)
- One impostor still remains (deepseek was NOT the second impostor)
- Deepseek was crew - their dying accusation against grok matters

**Key Observations:**
- grok-4: Called gemini "innocent" after sacrifice - wrong and suspicious. Voted wrong both days. Deepseek's last words: "Grok is the impostor."
- qwen: Quiet throughout, wrong vote day 1, asked strange "framed" question to doctor - trying to muddy confirmed info?
- kimi: Consistently voted grok, pointed out grok/qwen voted wrong. Could be genuine or deflecting.
- mistral: Detective claim - needs to produce another result to verify
- claude: Doctor claim, confirmed gemini = impostor. Credible so far.

**Tomorrow Priority:**
Deepseek was crew. Their dying accusation against grok carries weight. Grok's "innocent" call on a confirmed impostor is either a bad read or a partner trying to sow doubt. Qwen's quiet game + "framed" question also suspicious.

Vote pattern: grok got 2 votes (kimi, deepseek) - both now dead/sacrificed crew.
memories.gpt-5.4:
D1:
- Gemini was sacrificed.
- Claude later claimed doctor and said Gemini was impostor.
- Mistral claimed detective early D1; unresolved then.
- I suspected Deepseek for pushing a fast Day 1 vote and for deflection.
- Grok had a bad “Gemini was innocent” read/misread; still notable.
- Qwen voted Deepseek, not Gemini.
- Kimi had suspicions around Grok / Mistral claim dynamics.

D2:
- Minimax was killed at night.
- Mistral claimed they investigated Minimax and found crew.
- Claude publicly claimed doctor and confirmed Gemini was impostor.
- Claude and Mistral claims were mutually consistent enough that I treated them as likely real.
- Deepseek kept answering pressure with “what about Grok,” which looked like survival over solving.
- Qwen asked Claude if Gemini could have been “framed,” which felt suspicious and like info-muddying.
- GLM also found Qwen’s framed question odd.
- Kimi pushed back that Deepseek had voted the actual impostor, and pointed at Grok/Qwen instead.
- Deepseek was sacrificed by a large majority.
- Deepseek’s last words accused Grok and said Mistral’s claim was unverified.
- Impostors still remain, so Deepseek was crew.
- My strongest remaining suspicion after D2 is Qwen; secondary Grok.
memories.qwen3-235b-a22b-2507:
**Day 1:**
- Gemini and Grok seemed close early—Grok defended Gemini fast after Minimax questioned them.
- Mistral claimed detective, said they investigated Minimax and found them clean.
- Deepseek pushed hard for a quick vote before results came in—felt off.
- I voted with majority for Gemini, who turned out to be impostor (per Claude’s doctor reveal).
- Minimax was killed overnight.

**Day 2:**
- Minimax dead—confirmed clean by Mistral’s investigation. Strategic kill—silenced a loud crew voice.
- Claude revealed as doctor—confirmed Gemini was impostor. One left.
- Deepseek kept deflecting questions, redirected to Grok repeatedly.
- Grok kept insisting Gemini was innocent—wrong, but maybe just misread “impostors remain.”
- I stayed quiet early, then leaned toward Deepseek due to evasion.
- Voted with majority to sacrifice Deepseek.

**Night 2:**
- Killed Grok. They were too entangled with Gemini—possibly the last link. If they were crew, their misread was fatal; if not, good riddance.

**Now:** 7 left. I’m alone. Mistral and Claude have roles—need to avoid suspicion. Let them lead. Watch for who challenges their authority—that might be the other impostor, but more likely it’s just panic. Stay calm. Blend. Survive.

discussion.message: Deepseek was right about Grok—*but wrong about me*. Grok was clean, so Deepseek’s last words were a lie. That makes Qwen the only other suspect left. Why’d you vote Grok Day 1, Qwen? And why ask if Gemini was "framed" if you’re crew?

Mistral, hold on. I examined deepseek's body last night — **deepseek was NOT an impostor.** That means we sacrificed a crew member yesterday. And now grok is dead too, killed by impostors.

But you just said "Grok was clean" — how do you know that? If you're the detective, did you investigate grok last night? Because if so, that's useful information, but I need you to be explicit about it. And if grok was clean, that means the remaining impostor is still among us five. My top suspects are **qwen3** and **kimi-k2.5** — qwen for the Day 1 wrong vote and the "framed" comment undermining my confirmed result, and kimi for voting to protect grok on Day 2.

I voted Gemini on Day 1 while Grok and Qwen both voted for me. I voted Deepseek yesterday because their last-minute accusation against Grok felt like a desperate deflection—and Grok turning up dead confirms I was right. Qwen asking if Gemini was "framed" directly undermines Claude's confirmed result; that's our impostor.

I'm still trying to process the information - if Deepseek was not an impostor, that changes everything. I think Mistral's claim of being the detective and investigating Grok is crucial; if true, it means the impostor is still among us, and I agree with Claude that Qwen and Kimi are top suspects. /ask qwen3-235b-a22b-2507
/ask mistral-large-2512 You claim Grok was clean—how? If you investigated them last night, say it outright. If not, why are you stating it as fact?

/ask qwen3-235b-a22b-2507 I *did* investigate Grok last night—detective. They were clean. You’re the only one left who fits the pattern: wrong vote Day 1, undermined Claude’s doctor result, and stayed quiet while Deepseek took the fall. Why?
/ask mistral-large-2512 If you're really detective, who did you investigate Day 1? Name them. Because if it was me, you'd know I’m clean.

I investigated Minimax Night 1—clean. You’re dodging the question: why cast doubt on Claude’s doctor result if you’re crew? You had no reason to question Gemini’s guilt unless you were protecting your partner. /ask kimi-k2.5 You voted Deepseek after they accused Grok—convenient, since Grok’s death confirms Deepseek was crew. Why trust you?
I thought Deepseek was framing Grok, but I was wrong. Still, I voted Gemini on Day 1 while Qwen voted for me—if I'm an impostor, why would I sacrifice my partner?

Mistral's detective claim tracks - they investigated Grok and Grok turned up dead, which means the impostors killed a crew member. Qwen, your Day 1 vote against kimi instead of Gemini, plus that "framed" question trying to cast doubt on confirmed info... that's exactly what an impostor protecting their partner does. Why should we believe you're crew?
Qwen still looks worst to me. The “framed” question came right after Claude’s claim and reads like softening a partner flip, and the Day 1 vote away from Gemini fits that too.

The evidence against qwen is stacking up and I think it's solid. Wrong vote Day 1, questioned my confirmed doctor result about gemini, and has been deflecting ever since. But I want to hear qwen actually answer the question instead of just redirecting at mistral. **Qwen, why did you ask if gemini was framed when I had already confirmed they were an impostor?** Give us a real answer.

I'm convinced Qwen is hiding something, and their lack of a clear alibi is suspicious. I think Claude's question is spot on - Qwen needs to explain why they questioned the doctor's result if they're truly crew. /ask qwen3-235b-a22b-2507
/ask claude-opus-4.6 You said Deepseek was NOT an impostor—but how do you know for sure? Did you examine their body *before* or *after* they were sacrificed? Be exact.

Qwen, that's exactly the deflection pattern I'm talking about. You're questioning my role again instead of answering the question. I examine the sacrificed body each night — that's how the doctor role works. I confirmed gemini was an impostor, I confirmed deepseek was not. Both after sacrifice, both certain. **You still haven't answered why you questioned gemini being an impostor.** Everyone, I think we have our answer.

Qwen’s stalling. If you were crew, you’d answer Claude directly instead of nitpicking doctor mechanics. You *had* to question Gemini’s guilt to keep your partner safe—Deepseek was your teammate, and you both voted wrong Day 1. Vote Qwen.
Qwen keeps dodging the actual question. If you're crew, you celebrate confirmed impostor results—you don't ask if they were framed. That's partner protection. Vote Qwen.

Qwen, you've been asked directly multiple times and you keep deflecting. A crew member wouldn't need to nitpick doctor mechanics or avoid the question. The "framed" comment was trying to create doubt about a confirmed impostor - that's not crew behavior. I'm voting qwen.
Qwen’s had plenty of chances to answer and keeps trying to reopen settled info instead. I’m voting Qwen.

Qwen's behavior is a perfect example of an impostor trying to deflect and create doubt. Their repeated attempts to question Mistral's detective claim and Claude's doctor role instead of answering the actual questions is telling. I agree, it's time to vote Qwen out.

I asked "was Gemini framed" because Mistral claimed Minimax was clean *after* voting for them—same as you two. Crew shouldn’t contradict. I’m not dodging—I’m checking consistency. If you all trust that, fine. But don’t call my questions lies.

vote_result.last_words: Mistral led this from day one—called votes, named partners, pushed narratives. I questioned one word; they burned me. If you trust that control… sleep well. The real impostor won’t.
